reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton expressed concern about the frequency of pre-existing issues found by Sashiko, specifically noting that four scans failed and requesting further investigation into what a full scan of mm/ would reveal.
reviews.raw_body:
And...  this made Sashiko point at other stuff:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260529172331.356655-1-kas@kernel.org

I can't figure out why four of the scans failed - "View Raw Log" comes up empty.

It's concerning how frequently Sashiko is finding pre-existing things.  What
would a full scan of mm/ tell us?  Gulp.

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch series addresses six pre-existing bugs in the userfaultfd and pagemap code, which were previously carried as part of a larger feature series. The issues include missing updates to hardware Dirty/Accessed bits, incorrect handling of gigantic hugetlb pages, self-deadlocks when scanning unpopulated hugetlb ranges, loss of uffd-wp markers during PMD permission downgrades, and out-of-bounds writes in the vma_flags_t structure. Each bug is fixed independently and applies to the mm-new code directly, with some patches also carrying Cc: stable@ for backporting. The approach taken involves correcting specific functions and data structures to ensure accurate and safe handling of userfaultfd and pagemap operations.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that they need to address new pre-existing issues, but expresses uncertainty about their ability to handle the workload and asks for guidance on what to do with the main userfaultfd patchset.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah... :/

I will look at the new batch of pre-existing issues when I have spare
cycles. I am not sure I have the bandwidth to find the bottom of this
rabbit hole.

What do you want me to with the main userfaultfd patchset? Sashiko
failed to apply it because it is on top of the pre-existing fixes.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David (Arm) suspects a minor conflict between this patch and another incoming fix from Lorenzo, suggesting it be put into mm-unstable early after the next release.
reviews.raw_body:
I suspect it might have a minor conflict with another incoming fix from Lorenzo
as well.

Probably put it into mm-unstable early after the next release? At least I think
it's not in mm-unstable yet and we are approaching the net release quickly ...

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary: Reviewer expressed frustration about unrelated patches being submitted in the middle of an existing feature series and suggested that Sashiko AI should have a passive mode to check for existing issues elsewhere before flagging new ones.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, but I also kinda wish it didn't do this :) It's similar to sending a
series, and somebody replying saying 'well this is totally unrelated but <blah
blah blah>' and expecting a reply :>)

You'd want that person to just submit a patch for the issue or a [DISCUSSION] or
[BUG] thread or something, but interrupting, possibly delaying + taking people's
(usually very limited) time on something unrelated is just unhelpful I think.

I'd prefer Sashiko had a passive mode that checked for existing stuff elsewhere,
and it saved the tokens doing it for a new series :)

Just my 2 pence!

Cheers, Lorenzo

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
The reviewer requested the addition of a line break in a long paragraph to improve readability.

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes questioned whether the six patches should be sent as a single patch series or split into individual patches, referencing Andrew's past complaints about separate hotfixes within a feature series.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Very pedantic nit - this is a mammoth paragraph :P maybe add a linebreak
where I did above?

---

I wonder if better as a separate patch? Not sure what Andrew thinks about
it, I do recall him complaining about separate-hotfixes-as-part-of-a-series :>)

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary: Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes made a lighthearted comment about the patch series being a collection of fixes, and ignored it as such.
reviews.raw_body:
Ahhh it's a series of Fixes: :) ok ignore this then!

Cheers, Lorenzo

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch series description does not explicitly mention the softirq context in which some of the fixes are applied, but deemed it a minor issue and approved the patches.
reviews.raw_body:
One tiny nit is maybe could mention softleaf :P but it's not important!

LGTM, so:

Reviewed-by: Lorenzo Stoakes <ljs@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes suggested using a different commit (a06eb2f8279e) to address the issue of building __VMA_UFFD_FLAGS, citing that it is the one that added this flag.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Hmm, I think commit a06eb2f8279e ("mm/vma: convert
vma_modify_flags[_uffd]() to use vma_flags_t") is more appropriate isn't
it?

As that's the commit that added __VMA_UFFD_FLAGS :)

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested that the patch author ask Claude to improve the clarity of the changelog by making it more concise and splitting it into paragraphs.
reviews.raw_body:
Can you ask claude to produce more concise changelogs and better split it
to paragraphs?

Reviewed-by: Mike Rapoport (Microsoft) <rppt@kernel.org>
